title: pavonia malvacea
intro: Pavonia malvacea, also known as the tropical hibiscus or rose mallow, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Pavonia malvacea have been used in herbal medicine, valued for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to help with wound healing and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pavonia malvacea include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea, infused in hot water, or used as a topical poultice to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
